concepts.title: The Interview is a short story by R.K. Narayan
concepts.description: The story revolves around a conversation between Dr. Robert and a young boy who is an interviewee. The doctor's intention is to test the boy's moral values and knowledge, but the boy's responses lead to a series of misunderstandings and revelations.

concepts.title: Theme of the story
concepts.description: The theme of the story is the clash between traditional and modern values, and the struggle to understand each other's perspectives.

concepts.title: Character Analysis of the Interviewer
concepts.description: The interviewer, Dr. Robert, is a symbol of Western values and morality. He is portrayed as a sophisticated and educated person, but his questions reveal his own biases and lack of understanding of Indian culture and values.

concepts.title: Key Events in the Story
concepts.description: The boy is asked about his father's occupation, and he replies that his father is a 'village man'. The doctor is puzzled and asks him to explain, but the boy says that his father is a 'village man' because he lives in a village. This leads to a series of humorous misunderstandings.

concepts.title: Identifying the Boy's Father
concepts.description: Students often assume that the boy's father is a traditional Indian man, but in reality, the boy's father is a 'village man' because he lives in a village, not because of any cultural or social connotations.

concepts.title: Key Quotes from the Story
concepts.description: Some important quotes from the story include: 'What is your father's occupation?' 'He is a village man.' 'But how does that happen?' 'He lives in a village.'

concepts.title: Symbolism in the Story
concepts.description: The story is full of symbolism, with the village representing tradition and the city representing modernity. The doctor's questions and the boy's responses are also symbolic of the clash between these two worlds.

concepts.title: Author's Purpose
concepts.description: The author, R.K. Narayan, uses the story to highlight the differences between traditional and modern values, and to show how these differences can lead to misunderstandings and conflicts.
